We implemented zero-trust principles, standardized DevSecOps pipelines, and improved Authority to Operate timelines by 30%.

help_articles.title: Do you help with SAM.gov registration?
help_articles.body:
Yes. Our team provides both guided and full-service assistance with SAM.gov registration.

What we can help with:
- Determining whether you need an active SAM.gov registration and which entity to register
- Preparing required information (legal name, banking details, tax information, ownership structure)
- Walking through the entity validation and UEI assignment process
- Troubleshooting common errors and validation issues
- Reviewing your registration before final submission

Engagement options:
- **Guided support (done-with-you):** A SAM specialist walks you through the registration screens via remote sessions.
- **Full-service support (done-for-you):** We prepare the registration details based on your documentation and coordinate updates with you for final submission.

To request help:
1. Use the "Still need help? Contact an expert" button on this page.
2. Select **"SAM.gov Registration Support"** as the topic.
3. Provide your target deadline and any current roadblocks (for example, entity validation errors).

We typically respond within one business day to schedule a short scoping call and confirm the right level of support for your business.

help_articles.title: How long does SAM.gov registration usually take?
help_articles.body:
SAM.gov registration timelines vary depending on entity validation, bank account verification, and internal review.

Typical timelines:
- **Simple cases:** 13 weeks from start to active status
- **With entity validation issues:** 48 weeks, especially for new entities or recent name changes

Tips to avoid delays:
- Make sure your legal name, address, and tax ID match IRS and state records
- Confirm your bank account details and routing numbers before entering them
- Respond quickly to any validation emails from SAM.gov or supporting systems

If you are facing a hard deadline (for example, a specific solicitation), we recommend starting at least 6 weeks prior. Our "SAM.gov Registration Assistance" service can help resolve issues more quickly when they arise.

help_articles.title: What information do I need before starting SAM.gov registration?
help_articles.body:
Before starting SAM.gov registration, gather:

Business details:
- Legal business name and any doing-business-as (DBA) names
- Physical and mailing addresses
- Taxpayer Identification Number (TIN or EIN)
- UEI (if already assigned) or prior DUNS information if applicable

Banking information:
- Bank routing number and account number used for federal payments
- Bank contact details (sometimes requested for verification)

Ownership and management:
- Names, titles, and ownership percentages for key principals
- Contact information for the government point of contact

You should also have:
- Access to the email address you use for login and validation steps
- A clear understanding of your business type (for example, LLC, corporation, sole proprietor)

Having this information ready will significantly reduce the time needed to complete your SAM.gov registration.

help_articles.title: How do I update my payment method for advisory plans?
help_articles.body:
You can update your payment method for ongoing advisory plans at any time.

Steps:
1. Log in to your client portal.
2. Navigate to **Billing & Payments**.
3. Click **Update payment method**.
4. Enter your new card or ACH information and save.

If your payment method fails, we will automatically retry a few times before pausing services. If you need help or prefer to update payment details via a secure link, contact our billing team using the **Contact** page or this Help Center.

help_articles.title: Where can I find my invoices and receipts?
help_articles.body:
Invoices and receipts are available in your client portal.

To access them:
1. Log in to your account.
2. Go to **Billing & Payments**.
3. Select the **Invoices** tab.
4. Download PDF copies of any invoice or receipt.

For workshop and webinar registrations purchased without a client portal account, a copy of your receipt is emailed to the address used at checkout.

help_articles.title: How do I use the Capability Statement Builder?
help_articles.body:
Our Capability Statement Builder lets you create a polished PDF in minutes.

Quick start:
1. Open **Tools e Capability Statement Builder** from the main navigation.
2. Choose a template (for example, **IT & Cybersecurity**).
3. Enter your company information, NAICS codes, core competencies, and past performance.
4. Select your target agencies and a color theme (for example, **Blue Federal**).
5. Click **Generate Preview** to see your capability statement on screen.

You can download the PDF or save your information to edit later. Use the **Using Tools & Portals** category for additional how-to guides.

help_articles.title: How do I save workshops to my learning plan?
help_articles.body:
You can build a learning plan by saving workshops you�d1�92re interested in.

On the **Training & Workshops** page:
1. Use filters such as **Skill Level** and **Topic/Track**.
2. On any workshop card, click **Save to Learning Plan**.
3. Repeat for additional workshops.

To see your plan:
- Click **Learning Plan** in the site header.
- From there, you can remove workshops or view the total estimated cost.

Your saved learning plan is stored in your browser and/or account, so you can come back and refine it over time.

help_articles.title: How does the Reading List work?
help_articles.body:
The Reading List lets you save articles to review later.

To add an article:
1. Go to **Insights & Resources e Articles**.
2. Click the **Save to Reading List** icon on any article card.

To view saved items:
- Click **Reading List** in the site header.

Saved items remain associated with your browser or account until you remove them.

help_articles.title: What is the Consultation Plan and how do I use it?
help_articles.body:
The Consultation Plan helps you organize the services you want to discuss before meeting with our team.

How to use it:
1. Browse **Services** and click **Add to Consultation Plan** on any service card.
2. Open the **Consultation Plan** from the header to see your selected services.
3. Click **Schedule Consultation** to choose a date, time, and share notes about your goals.

This allows our consultants to prepare and ensure your session covers all selected services.

help_articles.title: How quickly will someone respond to my Help Center request?
help_articles.body:
We aim to respond to most Help Center requests within one business day.

Typical response times:
- **Registration & Compliance topics (including SAM.gov):** same or next business day
- **Billing & Payments:** within 2 business days
- **General questions:** within 2 business days

If your request is urgent (for example, a solicitation with an imminent due date), please mention your deadline in the message so we can prioritize appropriately.

help_articles.title: Can I reschedule my consultation?
help_articles.body:
Yes. You can reschedule consultations up to 24 hours before the scheduled start time.

To reschedule:
1. Open your confirmation email and click **Manage Appointment**.
2. Choose a new date and time that works for you.

If you need to reschedule within 24 hours, contact us using the **Contact** page or reply to your confirmation email. We�d1�92ll do our best to accommodate changes.

services.name: Quick-Look Proposal Compliance Check (Small Business)
services.shortDescription: Fast, fixed-fee compliance review of your federal proposal before submission.
services.longDescription: Designed for small businesses under 50 employees, this quick-look review focuses on compliance and responsiveness. We check your proposal against the RFP instructions, evaluation criteria, and submission requirements, highlighting any gaps or risks so you can fix issues before submission.

services.name: Small Business Proposal Tune-Up & Review
services.shortDescription: In-depth review of structure, messaging, and compliance for one federal proposal.
services.longDescription: This service combines compliance checking with strategic feedback on clarity, win themes, and differentiation. Ideal for small businesses submitting to federal agencies for the first time or trying to improve low win rates.

services.name: Enterprise Red Team Proposal Review
services.shortDescription: Full-scale red team review for complex, high-value federal proposals.
services.longDescription: For mid-sized and large contractors pursuing complex, multi-volume bids. Includes compliance review, competitive assessment, and structured red team sessions with detailed feedback.

services.name: Federal Readiness Assessment
services.shortDescription: Holistic assessment of your federal contracting readiness tailored to small businesses.
services.longDescription: We evaluate your registrations, certifications, pipeline, past performance, and internal processes to determine how ready you are to pursue and win federal contracts. You receive a written roadmap with prioritized actions for the next 63 months.

services.name: Capture Strategy Blueprint
services.shortDescription: Structured capture strategy for 13 priority federal opportunities.
services.longDescription: A focused engagement to build a capture strategy blueprint, including customer analysis, stakeholder mapping, competitive positioning, teaming approach, and action plan through proposal submission. Tailored for smaller firms that need a repeatable capture framework.

services.name: Pipeline Prioritization Sprint
services.shortDescription: Two-week sprint to score and prioritize your federal opportunity pipeline.
services.longDescription: We apply a structured scoring model to your current opportunity list, facilitate a bid/no-bid working session, and leave you with a prioritized pipeline and decision framework you can reuse.

services.name: Proposal Writing Support
services.shortDescription: Hands-on help drafting compliant, compelling proposal sections for targeted federal bids.
services.longDescription: We work alongside your team to outline, draft, and refine key proposal sections. Support can include management approach, technical response, past performance write-ups, and resumes, focusing on clarity and alignment to evaluation criteria.

services.name: Proposal Color Team Review & Coaching
services.shortDescription: Facilitated color team review with coaching for your internal proposal team.
services.longDescription: We facilitate a structured color team review (pink, red, or gold as appropriate) and provide coaching to your team on how to run effective reviews in the future. Includes a written summary of key findings and actions.

services.name: SAM.gov Registration Assistance (Done-With-You)
services.shortDescription: Guided working sessions to complete your initial SAM.gov registration correctly.
services.longDescription: Ideal for small businesses registering in SAM.gov for the first time. A registration specialist walks you through the process, screenshare-by-screenshare, helping you avoid common errors and delays.

services.name: SAM.gov Registration Full-Service Setup
services.shortDescription: End-to-end management of your SAM.gov registration, including issue resolution.
services.longDescription: We prepare and submit your SAM.gov registration on your behalf, coordinate responses to validation requests, and troubleshoot issues until your registration is active.

services.name: GSA Schedule Readiness Assessment
services.shortDescription: Assess whether your company is ready to pursue a GSA Schedule and what it will take to win.
services.longDescription: We review your past performance, pricing, internal systems, and marketing approach to determine if a GSA Schedule is a good fit. You receive a written readiness report and go/no-go recommendation.

services.name: GSA Schedule Offer Development Support
services.shortDescription: Hands-on support preparing your initial GSA Schedule offer package.
services.longDescription: We help you gather documentation, develop pricing, complete required forms, and respond to clarification questions from the contracting officer for your first GSA Schedule offer.

services.name: 8(a) Certification Application Coaching
services.shortDescription: Coaching and review support to prepare a stronger 8(a) certification application.
services.longDescription: We guide you through the SBA 8(a) application process, including eligibility review, documentation preparation, and narrative development. Includes up to two full reviews of your application package before submission.

services.name: Registration & Compliance Strategy Session
services.shortDescription: Targeted strategy session to plan your registration and compliance roadmap.
services.longDescription: In a 90-minute remote session, we map out your required registrations (SAM.gov, SBA, set-asides), basic cybersecurity obligations, and a realistic timeline that supports your federal revenue goals.
